title: Future of Humanity
whatItIs:
The future of humanity is the study of long-run trajectories for human life: more intelligence, more coordination, longer lives, new forms of work, and possibly new kinds of personhood.

The topic is speculative, but it becomes practical when you think about technologies already changing human capability.
whyItMatters:
It matters because many decisions today are really bets on what humans will be able to do tomorrow. Education, energy, media, and infrastructure all have future-shaped assumptions hidden inside them.

The topic also matters ethically, because the future is not just a technical outcome; it is a moral inheritance.
frontier: The frontier is whether civilisation can grow more powerful without becoming more brittle. AI, space, biotech, and governance are all part of that question.
openQuestions: What kind of civilisation do we want to build?
Which technologies change human nature itself?
How much should the present sacrifice for the future?
